prompt: Photorealistic sports photography of a high school football offensive lineman executing a pancake block during a Friday night game. The lineman wears a crimson red jersey (#CC0022) with white number 79, white football pants with red piping, and a matte black helmet with a red stripe. He is 6 foot 4, 285 pounds, built like a Division I prospect — thick legs, wide shoulders, barrel chest. Camera angle: low, shot from turf level looking upward at a dramatic 15-degree angle, emphasizing the player's mass and dominance over the defender being driven into the ground. The lineman's hands are locked on the defender's chest plate, fully extended arms, back flat, hips sunk low, textbook drive-block technique with the defender's feet leaving the turf. Shallow depth of field, f/2.0, isolating the collision from the background. Stadium lights create a halo of warm white light behind the player, with visible light rays cutting through wisps of breath vapor in the cold Wisconsin autumn air. The field is natural grass, slightly muddy, showing cleat marks and torn-up divots. A sideline chain-link fence and blurred red-clad teammates are barely visible in the bokeh background. Visible details: grass stains on the white pants, sweat on the forearms, turf particles frozen mid-air from the impact. The face is obscured by the helmet facemask and shadow — no identifiable features visible. Color palette skews toward deep reds, warm tungsten stadium light, and cold blue-black shadows. Shot on Canon EOS R3, 70-200mm f/2.8, 1/1600 shutter speed, ISO 6400. The image conveys raw power, controlled violence, and the unmistakable authority of a dominant lineman. Aspect ratio 16:9, landscape orientation.
notes: This is the most critical asset. The hero section uses a 70% dark gradient overlay (from-[#0A0A0A]/70 via-[#0D1117]/60 to-[#0A0A0A]/90). The image must read clearly through that overlay — high contrast between the red jersey and the dark background is essential. The subject should be positioned slightly right of center, leaving negative space on the left for the text overlay (JACOB / RODGERS headline). The bottom third of the image should be darker to support the measurables bar and scroll indicator.

prompt: Cinematic wide-angle photograph of a high school football stadium at night during pregame warmups, shot from the press box level looking down at a 30-degree angle toward the field. The stadium is a mid-size Wisconsin suburban facility — aluminum bleachers with modest capacity, a red running track surrounding the field, a basic scoreboard glowing in the distance. The field lights are fully on, casting harsh pools of warm white light that create sharp geometric shadows on the synthetic turf. A thin layer of ground fog or mist sits just above the field surface, catching the light and creating an ethereal, atmospheric quality. Small groups of players in dark silhouette are scattered across the field stretching and running routes — too far away and too dark to identify, just shapes suggesting the ritual of pregame. The sky is deep navy blue transitioning to black at the top of the frame, with a faint purple-orange glow lingering at the horizon from a recently set sun. Red banners or pennants on the fence line catch a hint of light. The overall mood is anticipatory, quiet before the storm — the calm tension before kickoff. No crowd visible, just empty bleachers catching stadium light reflections. Shot on Sony A7 IV, 24mm f/1.8, ISO 3200. Color grade: desaturated, cool blue shadows with warm tungsten highlights, crushed blacks. The image should feel almost monochromatic when an 80% dark overlay is applied — just shapes and light. Aspect ratio 16:9, landscape orientation.
notes: This sits behind the Film Reel section which uses an 80% opacity dark overlay (bg-[#0A0A0A]/80). The image needs to be subtle enough to function purely as texture — the video player and stats are the focal point. Avoid any bright hot spots that would bleed through the overlay. The mist/fog is key to creating atmospheric depth without competing with foreground content.

prompt: Photorealistic photograph of a teenage high school athlete performing a heavy conventional deadlift in a dimly lit, industrial-style training facility. The athlete is large-framed — 6 foot 4, 285 pounds, built like an offensive lineman — gripping a barbell loaded with four plates per side (approximately 405 pounds). He is at the mid-pull position: the bar has just passed his knees, his back is flat, his traps are engaged, his face is strained with effort and looking slightly downward. He wears a plain black athletic t-shirt, red compression shorts visible below, white Nike training shoes, and a thick leather powerlifting belt. His forearms are thick, his hands chalked white, with chalk dust visibly floating in the air around the bar. The training facility has exposed concrete floors with rubber mats, a black iron squat rack visible behind him, chains hanging from hooks on a pegboard wall, and a worn American flag on the far wall. A single overhead industrial pendant light with an exposed Edison bulb creates a dramatic pool of warm golden light directly above the lift, casting deep shadows that sculpt the athlete's arms, back, and legs. The rest of the gym fades to near-black. A faint red LED accent light strip along the baseboards provides a subtle crimson glow on the concrete floor. The camera is positioned at hip height, slightly to the right at a 20-degree angle, creating a three-quarter view that shows both the strain on the athlete's body and the loaded barbell. Shallow depth of field, the background rack and wall are soft focus. Moisture and sweat are visible on the athlete's arms. The athlete's face is partially obscured by the downward angle and shadow from the overhead light — no identifiable facial features. Shot on Nikon Z9, 35mm f/1.4, 1/500 shutter speed, ISO 2000. The mood is solitary, gritty, monastic — a teenager doing the work that nobody sees. Aspect ratio 16:9, landscape orientation.
notes: This maps to the Origin Story / The Work section which tracks Jacob's training trajectory from age 12 to present (730+ sessions). The section uses an 85% dark overlay. The image should reinforce the 'grind' narrative — solitary work, early mornings, no shortcuts. The red accent lighting subtly ties to the Pewaukee color system without being overt. The 405-lb deadlift is a real stat from Jacob's profile (age 14) and grounds the image in truth.

prompt: Photorealistic photograph of a high school football team in a tight sideline huddle during a night game, shot from within the huddle at shoulder height looking inward. Six to eight players in crimson red jerseys with white numbers are packed tightly together, their arms interlocked or draped over each other's shoulder pads. Their helmets are off, held at their sides or resting on their hips. The huddle is intensely close — the physicality of football brotherhood. The camera captures the torsos, arms, shoulder pads, and the circle of jerseys — faces are either turned away, looking down, or obscured by the low angle and helmet shadows. One player's hand grips a teammate's shoulder pad with white-knuckled intensity. The lighting is dramatic: harsh stadium lights from above and behind create a strong backlight that rims the players' helmets and shoulder pads with a white-gold edge light, while the front of the huddle is lit only by ambient bounce. Visible details: scuffed helmets, grass-stained pants, strips of athletic tape on wrists, a playsheet wristband. The background is completely blown out by the stadium lights — pure white bokeh fading to warm amber. The ground beneath them is trampled sideline grass. The color palette is dominated by the crimson red of the jerseys against the cool blue-black shadows of the bodies and the warm backlight. There is a slight motion blur on one player's arm suggesting animated conversation or a pre-play ritual. The mood is brotherhood, accountability, collective intensity — this is a team that fights for each other. Shot on Canon EOS R5, 24mm f/1.4, ISO 8000, 1/320 shutter speed. Aspect ratio 16:9, landscape orientation.
notes: The Character section uses a 90% dark overlay and presents three traits: Coachable, Team First, Relentless. This image sits behind those cards and a coach quote blockquote. At 90% overlay, only the strongest highlights and the red jersey color will be visible — which is the point. The red jersey fragments bleeding through the overlay will create a subtle but powerful emotional anchor. The shoulder-level angle is critical: it places the viewer inside the huddle, creating the sense that Jacob belongs to something larger than himself.

prompt: Photorealistic sports photography of a large, powerful high school athlete in the final release phase of a discus throw during a spring track and field meet. The athlete is 6 foot 4, 285 pounds — unmistakably a football player competing in track, not a typical lean thrower. He wears a red sleeveless track singlet with white side panels and black compression shorts. His body is fully rotated, his throwing arm extended behind him at the moment of release, the discus just leaving his fingertips with visible spin. His non-throwing arm is pulled tight against his chest for rotational speed. The athlete's legs are planted wide in the concrete throwing circle, his front foot braced against the toe board, his back leg driving. Every muscle in his forearm, shoulder, and obliques is visible under the skin — the effort is total. The setting is a high school track and field complex on a bright overcast spring day in Wisconsin: a cinder track visible in the background, a green grass infield, portable metal bleachers, and a chain-link safety cage partially framing the throw. The sky is a flat, bright white-gray overcast typical of early April in the upper Midwest. The camera is at ground level outside the throwing circle, shooting upward at a 20-degree angle from the 7 o'clock position relative to the thrower, capturing the full extension of the throw and the explosive torque of the body. Shallow depth of field renders the background into soft geometric shapes. The discus is sharp and frozen mid-flight with a slight motion trail. Chalk dust and dirt are visible on the athlete's hands and shoes. His face is turned away from the camera, following the discus, with only the back of his head and jawline visible. Shot on Canon EOS R3, 85mm f/1.4, 1/2500 shutter speed, ISO 400. The mood is explosive power translated from one sport to another — raw athleticism that proves this is not just a football player. Aspect ratio 16:9, landscape orientation.
notes: The Fit section uses an 88% dark overlay and presents three pitch blocks: Development Runway, What He's Looking For, and The Window. This image connects to Jacob's documented multi-sport profile (1st place discus, 1st place shot put). The track singlet in red ties to Pewaukee's colors. The overcast lighting differs intentionally from the night-game stadium shots elsewhere on the page — it signals a different context (spring track) while maintaining the same athlete profile. The ground-level camera angle is consistent with the hero shot's low perspective, creating visual coherence across sections.

prompt: Slow-motion cinematic footage of a high school football offensive lineman exploding off the line of scrimmage at the snap. The lineman wears a crimson red jersey with white number 79 and a matte black helmet. The camera is positioned at field level, slightly behind the line of scrimmage at a 45-degree angle, capturing the violent first step: the lineman's right foot drives off the turf, his left hand fires forward toward the defender's chest plate, his hips drop low in a powerful stance. Grass and rubber turf pellets erupt from the ground around his cleats in extreme slow motion, individual particles floating upward and catching the stadium light. The stadium lights are directly behind and above the player, creating a dramatic backlit silhouette with rim lighting that traces the contours of his helmet, shoulder pads, and arms in warm golden-white light. His face is completely obscured by the helmet facemask and the backlighting — he is a silhouette of force. Wisps of cold breath vapor trail from his facemask in slow motion. The background is a defocused wash of stadium lights, creating large circular bokeh orbs in warm amber and cool white. The opposing defender is barely visible as a dark mass on the right edge of frame, about to receive the impact. The motion is deliberate and powerful — each frame communicates barely contained violence. The footage plays at 240fps slowed to 24fps, creating a 10x slowdown effect. The color grade is deep and cinematic: crushed blacks, warm highlights, desaturated midtones, with the red jersey being the only saturated color in the frame. The clip is 5 seconds long and loops seamlessly — the action begins with the lineman in his stance and ends just after the first step and hand strike, before contact resolution. Camera: Phantom Flex4K, 85mm T1.3 cine lens. The mood is primal, unstoppable, inevitable — a force of nature.
notes: This is for Kling 3.0 image-to-video generation. Generate the first frame as a still image using the hero-action prompt, then use this prompt to animate it. The 5-second loop should feel seamless — consider generating a clip that begins and ends in a similar body position (set stance to first-step explosion) so the loop point is not jarring. The video sits behind the same 70% gradient overlay as the hero image. The slow-motion speed means we only need 2-3 seconds of actual motion to fill 5 seconds. Prioritize the turf particle explosion and breath vapor as the primary kinetic elements that sell the slow-motion effect.
